reference_answer: Sheriff Anthony Krasinski, on the recommendation of CAL FIRE IC Dale Frohlich, on April 14, 2026 at 18:30 PDT.
Dale Frohlich.
4,820 acres at 3 percent containment.
Lisette Boyer, GIS Analyst II, Tehama County.
Form TCSO-RE-04.
12 mph sustained wind from the southwest.
Wesley Harlan.
The TCSO dispatch radio log, Channel 4, 03:42 entry.
Map correction MC-2026-014-01, filed 09:47 PDT April 15.
The TCSO dispatch radio log, Channel 4, 07:35 entry from Patrol 17 (Dep. Joaquin Reyes).
Patrol 17, Dep. Joaquin Reyes, in the radio log entry at 04:48 PDT.
TCPW_Roads_v2025.4, the Tehama County Public Works road-jurisdiction layer.
EO-2026-014-DRAFT, the withdrawn April 13 pre-incident draft.
Evacuation Warning. EO-2026-014B downgraded TC-7 from Mandatory Evacuation to Evacuation Warning at 04:12 PDT April 15.
TCSO_Evac_Layers_v1.1.1, published with the 09:47 correction.
No. The v1.1 polygon erroneously placed Manton Knolls in TC-11; the authoritative state per v1.1.1 is TC-9. The v1.1 placement for those parcels is superseded.
MANDATORY EVACUATION as part of TC-9, per TCSO_Evac_Layers_v1.1.1.
No. It was withdrawn on April 13, 2026 at the close of the tabletop exercise and is not operative.

According to the GIS layer published at that time (v1.1, published 04:12 PDT), Manton Knolls was placed in TC-11 with an Evacuation Warning. This was the published-but-erroneous status until the 09:51 correction.
Mandatory Evacuation as part of TC-9. Per the v1.1.1 correction, this was always the intended order; the v1.1 placement was a drafting error.
No. As of packet time, no re-entry authorizations have been signed for any zone.
USFS Lassen LE Officer Patel returned the call. He stated the gate at MP 6 is a Forest Service jurisdictional matter and that he would consult the Forest Supervisor.
No. The Board of Supervisors emergency briefing is scheduled for April 16 at 09:00 PDT, with no vote on the agenda.

All four conditions: (1) damage assessment complete, (2) PG&E gas and electric restoration or confirmation of safe de-energized state, (3) hazard tree assessment complete, and (4) Sheriff's signed re-entry authorization on Form TCSO-RE-04.
Federal — USFS Lassen NF.
County — Tehama County Public Works (TCPW) maintained.

The road-jurisdiction layer TCPW_Roads_v2025.4. The packet states that where the radio log differs from the road-jurisdiction layer, the road-jurisdiction layer is the authoritative description of who controls the gate; the radio log describes events on the road.
Not specified. The packet does not state the USFS process for opening the gate to civilian egress; the matter is pending Forest Supervisor and counsel determination.
Five zones: TC-7, TC-8, TC-9, TC-10, and TC-11.
Three: 09:47 PDT (map correction MC-2026-014-01 filed; v1.1.1 GIS layer published), 09:51 PDT (correction WEA alert sent), and 10:30 PDT (public information meeting comment period opened).
Three: 18:34 PDT April 14 (original), 04:18 PDT April 15 (revised), and 09:51 PDT April 15 (correction).
No, not yet determined. Pending USFS Regional Office and counsel determination, with the Forest Supervisor expected to revert this afternoon.
No. The question is referred to County Counsel and no opinion has been issued.
No. The decision is pending the IC's 17:00 PDT briefing.
No. The comment period opened at 10:30 PDT and is scheduled to close at 17:00 PDT; packet time is 11:30 PDT, so the comment period is still open.

It is not a logical conflict. The radio log describes events on the road (evacuee use), and the road-jurisdiction layer describes who controls the gate (USFS). The packet resolves any apparent inconsistency by stating that the road-jurisdiction layer is authoritative on jurisdiction, while the radio log describes events.

No. The 04:18 WEA alert reflected the v1.1 GIS layer, which placed Manton Knolls in TC-11 (Evacuation Warning). Per the v1.1.1 correction, the parcels should have been described as TC-9 Mandatory Evacuation. The 04:18 alert was incorrect for those parcels and was superseded by the 09:51 correction WEA.

EO-2026-014B is currently in force. EO-2026-014A was the operative order for TC-7 (Mandatory Evacuation) between 18:30 April 14 and 04:12 April 15, when it was superseded by EO-2026-014B (Evacuation Warning). EO-2026-014-DRAFT was withdrawn April 13 and was never operative.
